options.label: SB 3 Buttons
options.description: A single-breasted waistcoat with three functioning buttons set low on the front, creating a deep, open V that exposes more shirt and tie. The minimal button count gives a relaxed, modern silhouette often paired with casual or contemporary tailoring. It reads less formal than the traditional five-button cut.

options.label: SB 4 Buttons
options.description: A single-breasted waistcoat with four buttons, offering a balanced opening that sits between the deep three-button cut and the high-buttoning five. It provides a clean, slightly open chest while still covering the waistband, suiting both business and semi-formal looks. The four-button stance is common on shawl-lapel and lapelled vests.

options.label: SB 5 Buttons
options.description: The classic single-breasted waistcoat with five buttons, the traditional and most common configuration for three-piece suits and morning dress. The higher button placement closes the chest neatly and frames the tie, giving a timeless, formal appearance. The bottom button is conventionally left undone.

options.label: SB 6 Buttons
options.description: A single-breasted waistcoat with six buttons, producing a high, closed front with closely spaced buttons for a fuller, more buttoned-up look. The increased button count requires the front to sit higher for even spacing and lends a vintage or fashion-forward character. On six-button cutaway styles the bottom button does not align and is left open.

options.label: DB 4 × 2
options.description: A double-breasted waistcoat with four buttons of which two fasten, giving an overlapping front with a compact, lower-buttoning closure. The wrapped front adds formality and visual width across the chest while keeping a relatively open, minimal look. It is a tidy, less ornate take on the double-breasted vest.

options.label: DB 6 × 2
options.description: A double-breasted waistcoat with six buttons of which two fasten, leaving the lower two buttons decorative on the cutaway. The arrangement creates a longer overlapping front with a slim fastening point, balancing the boldness of a DB front with a clean line. It is a refined choice for formal three-piece looks.

options.label: DB 6 × 3
options.description: A double-breasted waistcoat with six buttons of which three fasten, the traditional balanced DB configuration that fastens through most of the front. The keystone or straight button rows imply a V-shaped torso and lend a structured, formal presence. It is the classic double-breasted vest stance for morning dress and weddings.

options.label: DB 8 × 4
options.description: A double-breasted waistcoat with eight buttons of which four fasten, the most ornate and formal DB stance with two full rows down the front. The dense buttoning and broad overlap create a commanding, ceremonial look favored by English clothiers, often paired with shawl or peak lapels. It is a staple of Ascot and morning-dress dressing.

options.label: Regular
options.description: The standard waistcoat hem featuring two pointed tabs (a 'W' shape), one beneath each front, which is the most common daywear bottom. The points sit just below the waistband to ensure no shirt shows beneath the vest. This shape elongates the torso and reads as the default classic finish.

options.label: Small Point
options.description: A hem ending in a single shallow point at the center front, a subtler take on the pointed bottom with less dramatic projection. The gentle point keeps a tailored finish while looking cleaner and more restrained than full double points. It pairs well with slimmer, modern waistcoats.

options.label: Square / Flat
options.description: A straight, flat-cut hem with no points, giving the waistcoat a clean horizontal bottom edge. This streamlined finish reads modern and understated, and is also traditional on certain evening and morning-dress vests. The square hem simplifies the silhouette and works well under a buttoned jacket.

options.label: Round
options.description: A hem with rounded lower corners rather than sharp points, softening the front line of the waistcoat. The curved finish is more common on evening vests and lends a gentler, less angular appearance. It gives a slightly retro, formal-but-soft character.

options.label: Slant / Point
options.description: A hem cut on an angle that slants up toward the sides, ending in a single forward-projecting point at center. The diagonal lines draw the eye downward and inward, slimming and lengthening the torso. This dynamic cut gives the waistcoat a sharp, contemporary edge.

options.label: Machine Stitched
options.description: Precise machine buttonholes.

options.label: Handmade Buttonholes
options.description: Hand-stitched buttonholes and buttoning — bespoke standard.

options.label: Handmade Round Buttonholes
options.description: Handmade round front buttonholes.

options.label: Left over Right
options.description: Standard Western fastening.

options.label: Right over Left
options.description: Reverse fastening.

options.label: Full Canvas
options.description: Full canvas construction runs a floating horsehair-and-wool interlining through the entire front of the waistcoat, sewn rather than glued. It gives the best shape retention, natural drape, and longevity, molding to the body over time. This is the pinnacle of bespoke vest construction.

options.label: Half Canvas
options.description: Half canvas construction places a floating canvas interlining through the upper chest while the lower portion is fused, balancing structure with cost. It delivers a clean chest and good drape up top, lighter than full canvas and well suited to warmer wear. It is the popular middle-ground construction.

options.label: Unconstructed Chest
options.description: Unconstructed (unlined or minimally interlined) waistcoats omit stiffening canvas almost entirely, relying on the cloth itself for shape. The result is soft, lightweight, and breathable with a relaxed drape, ideal for summer and casual odd vests. It reads informal and gives the most fluid, unstructured feel.

options.label: Single Layer Chest Fusing
options.description: Single fused layer — crisp without bulk.

options.label: Fused Canvas
options.description: Fused construction laminates a synthetic interlining to the shell fabric with heat and adhesive, a fast and economical way to add stiffness. It keeps the front crisp initially but offers less breathability and a less natural drape than canvas, and can blister over time. It is the entry-level, lowest-cost option.

options.label: V-Neckline
options.description: A waistcoat with a deep V-shaped neckline and no lapels, the cleanest and most common opening for three-piece suits. The bare V exposes the shirt and tie and creates a long vertical line up the chest. It is versatile and reads as the default business and formal cut.

options.label: U-Neckline
options.description: A waistcoat with a low, U-shaped (horseshoe) neckline that opens wide and deep, exposing a large expanse of shirt front. Traditionally associated with formal evening and morning dress, it frames a bow tie or dress shirt elegantly. The scooped opening reads as the most formal and ceremonial neckline.

options.label: Notch without Collar Band
options.description: A waistcoat fitted with notch lapels, mirroring the small step-cut collar of a suit jacket on the vest front. The added lapels give the waistcoat more structure and visual weight, making it look like a jacket on its own. It suits standalone or odd-vest looks and lends a casual-tailored feel.

options.label: Peak without Collar Band
options.description: A waistcoat with peak lapels, whose pointed lapel tips angle upward toward the shoulders for a sharp, formal flourish. Peak lapels add the most assertive, dressy character to a vest and are often paired with double-breasted fronts. They give the waistcoat a bold, elevated presence.

options.label: Shawl without Collar Band
options.description: A waistcoat with a smooth, rounded shawl lapel that runs in one continuous curve with no notch or peak. The unbroken roll gives a soft, luxurious, and decidedly formal look, classic on evening and morning-dress waistcoats. It is the signature lapel of dressy double-breasted vests.

options.label: Notch with Collar Band
options.description: Notch lapel rising with stand — guard's vest.

options.label: Peak with Collar Band
options.description: Peak lapel with collar band — maximum formality.

options.label: Shawl with Collar Band
options.description: Shawl lapel with band — dinner jacket character.

options.label: Narrow 6 cm
options.description: Slim lapel.

options.label: Standard 7.5 cm
options.description: Medium width lapel.

options.label: Wide 9 cm
options.description: Wide lapel — bold statement.

options.label: No Buttonhole
options.description: Clean lapel.

options.label: Left
options.description: Left lapel buttonhole for flower.

options.label: Left Two
options.description: Two buttonholes on left lapel.

options.label: Right & Left
options.description: Buttonholes both lapels.

options.label: Regular Straight
options.description: Standard machine straight buttonhole.

options.label: Regular Round
options.description: Machine round-end buttonhole.

options.label: Real Straight
options.description: Handworked straight buttonhole.

options.label: Real Straight 0.5 cm
options.description: Handworked straight with 0.5 cm lip.

options.label: Matched Collar Felt
options.description: Felt matched to collar fabric.

options.label: Match Body Fabric
options.description: Collar felt matching main body.

options.label: No Chest Pocket
options.description: A waistcoat front with no breast pocket, leaving a clean uninterrupted upper panel. The absence of a chest pocket gives the most minimal, streamlined look and lets the fabric or pattern speak for itself. It suits sleek modern and formal vests where simplicity is desired.

options.label: Left Welt
options.description: A single welt breast pocket on the left chest, a slim raised slot finished with one strip of fabric. It is the classic waistcoat breast pocket, useful for a pocket watch or small accessory, and adds a subtle tailored detail to the upper front. The single welt reads traditional and understated.

options.label: Left Besom
options.description: A left besom (jetted) breast pocket finished with two narrow fabric lips and no welt, giving a flatter, more discreet opening than a welt. The double-piped slot reads cleaner and dressier, common on formal and evening waistcoats. It adds refinement without visual bulk on the chest.

options.label: Left Besom with Flap
options.description: Besom pocket with decorative flap.

options.label: Left Patch
options.description: A left patch breast pocket sewn onto the outside of the waistcoat front, the most casual and visible pocket style. Its applied, outlined shape adds texture and a relaxed, sporty character, common on tweed and odd waistcoats. The patch pocket signals informality and country tailoring.

options.label: Right & Left Welt
options.description: Welt breast pockets on both the left and right chest, mirroring each other across the upper front. The symmetrical pair gives a balanced, traditional four-pocket waistcoat look when combined with lower pockets. It reads classic and slightly more decorative than a single welt.

options.label: Right & Left Besom
options.description: Besom pockets both sides.

options.label: R&L Besom with Flap
options.description: Besom with flap both sides.

options.label: Right & Left Patch
options.description: Open patch pockets both sides.

options.label: No Lower Pockets
options.description: A waistcoat with no lower pockets, leaving the waist area of the front completely clean. The omission gives the sleekest, most minimal silhouette and emphasizes a smooth fabric front. It suits formal evening and fashion-forward vests where pockets would clutter the line.

options.label: Regular Welt
options.description: Two horizontal welt lower pockets at the waist, each finished with a single fabric strip. This is the traditional waistcoat lower pocket, sitting just above the points and useful for small items. The welt openings add a neat, classic tailored detail to the lower front.

options.label: 1.0 cm Width Welt
options.description: Narrow 1 cm welt — refined detail.

options.label: Besom
options.description: Two besom (jetted) lower pockets with double-piped lips and no welt, giving a flat, refined opening at the waist. The cleaner double-welt finish reads dressier than a single welt and is favored on formal and evening waistcoats. It keeps the lower front sleek and tailored.

options.label: Besom with Flap
options.description: Besom pocket with flap — traditional.

options.label: Patch
options.description: Two patch lower pockets sewn onto the outside of the waistcoat at the waist, the most casual and visible lower pocket option. Their applied, outlined shapes add texture and a rustic, country feel, common on tweed odd waistcoats. The patch style signals informality and ruggedness.

options.label: Slant Welt
options.description: Welt lower pockets cut on a diagonal slant, angling up toward the sides like hacking pockets. The slanted welts add movement and a sporting, equestrian character while remaining single-strip welts. They give the waistcoat a dynamic, country-tailoring edge.

options.label: Slant Besom
options.description: Besom lower pockets set on a slant, combining the flat double-piped opening with an angled, hacking-style orientation. The result is both dressy and dynamic, slimming the waist while keeping a clean line. It is a sophisticated detail bridging formal and sporting styles.

options.label: Slant Besom with Flap
options.description: Angled besom with flap.

options.label: None
options.description: No ticket pocket.

options.label: Besom
options.description: Besom ticket pocket.

options.label: Right Welt
options.description: Right-side welt ticket pocket.

options.label: Left Welt
options.description: Left-side welt ticket pocket.

options.label: Besom with Flap
options.description: Besom ticket pocket with flap.

options.label: Besom with Diamond Flap
options.description: Besom with decorative diamond flap.

options.label: Slant Besom
options.description: Angled besom ticket pocket.

options.label: Left & Right Slant Welt
options.description: Slant welt both sides.

options.label: Standard with Strap
options.description: The standard waistcoat back made of lining material with an adjustable strap and buckle at the center waist. The cinch allows the wearer to tighten or loosen the fit for comfort and to accommodate weight changes. It is the conventional, practical default for most suit waistcoats.

options.label: Without Belt
options.description: A waistcoat back without any belt or adjuster, giving a flat, uninterrupted rear panel. This relies on precise bespoke fitting since there is no means to adjust the waist, and yields the cleanest line under a jacket. It reads as the most tailored and minimal back treatment.

options.label: Matched Lining
options.description: A waistcoat with the back panel made in smooth lining fabric (often silk or satin), the traditional construction intended to be worn under a jacket. The slick lining reduces bulk and friction beneath the coat, and frequently carries the adjustable strap. It is the lightest, most conventional back for a suit vest.

options.label: Fabric
options.description: A waistcoat with the back made from the same suiting fabric as the front rather than lining, so it can be worn confidently without a jacket. The matching-cloth back gives a more substantial, finished look and is common on odd waistcoats and casual three-piece styles. It elevates the vest into a standalone garment.

options.label: Specify Fabric
options.description: Different specified fabric for back.

options.label: Specify Lining
options.description: Specified lining fabric for back.

options.label: DIY Printing
options.description: Custom print on back panel.

options.label: Fabric
options.description: Back belt in main body fabric.

options.label: Appointed Lining
options.description: Back belt in lining fabric.

options.label: With Strap & Buckle
options.description: Traditional cinching back strap with buckle.

options.label: Back Belt 3 cm Width + Buckle
options.description: Wide 3 cm back belt with buckle.

options.label: None
options.description: No contrast on lapel or collar.

options.label: DIY Contrast Lapel & Collar
options.description: Full custom contrast on lapel and collar.

options.label: DIY 1.0 cm Edging
options.description: 1 cm contrast edging on lapel and collar.

options.label: Specify Lapel
options.description: Specified contrast fabric on lapel.

options.label: Specify 1.0 cm Edging Lapel
options.description: Specified 1 cm edging on lapel.

options.label: Specify Collar
options.description: Specified contrast fabric on collar.

options.label: Specify 1.0 cm Edging Collar
options.description: Specified 1 cm edging on collar.

options.label: None
options.description: No pocket contrast.

options.label: DIY Flap Surface
options.description: Custom contrast on pocket flap surface.

options.label: DIY Seam
options.description: Custom contrast on pocket seam.

options.label: DIY 1.0 cm Contrast
options.description: Custom 1 cm contrast on pockets.

options.label: DIY Seam and Bottom
options.description: Custom contrast on pocket seam and bottom.

options.label: Specify Seam and Bottom
options.description: Specified contrast on pocket seam and bottom.

options.label: Specify Patch
options.description: Specified contrast on patch pocket.

options.label: Specify Patch Surface
options.description: Specified contrast on patch pocket surface.

options.label: Specify Seam
options.description: Specified contrast pocket seam only.

options.label: Match Fabric
options.description: Button covered in matching fabric.

options.label: DIY Fabric Covered Button
options.description: Custom fabric covered button.

options.label: Match Fabric
options.description: A lining chosen to match or closely tone with the waistcoat's shell fabric, giving a seamless, restrained interior and back. The tonal coordination reads understated and classic, drawing no attention away from the cloth itself. It is the safest, most traditional lining choice.

options.label: Navy Solid
options.description: A navy lining used on the back and interior of the waistcoat, a deep, versatile blue that pairs cleanly with most shell colors. It adds a quiet richness without being flashy, reading as a tasteful, slightly elevated alternative to black. Navy is a popular default for refined business and formal vests.

options.label: Black Solid
options.description: A black lining for the waistcoat's back and interior, the most formal and traditional choice, especially for evening and morning dress. Its neutral darkness disappears under a jacket and pairs with virtually any shell. Black lining reads sober, dressy, and conventional.

options.label: Cream / Ivory
options.description: A cream or ivory lining giving a soft, light interior and back, traditional on morning-dress and summer waistcoats. The pale tone reads fresh and warm, complementing off-white and light-toned shells. It lends a refined, ceremonial quality suited to weddings and Ascot.

options.label: Gold Paisley
options.description: A gold paisley patterned lining, a decorative silk-look interior with swirling teardrop motifs in warm gold tones. It adds a flash of personality and luxury revealed at the back or when the vest is open, contrasting an otherwise plain shell. This is the most expressive, statement-making lining option.

options.label: Custom Lining (Provide Code)
options.description: Custom color or pattern — specify at checkout.

options.label: Regular Lining
options.description: Standard lining construction.

options.label: Artistic Lining (Placement)
options.description: Printed placement lining — distinctive interior.

options.label: DIY Printing
options.description: Fully custom printed lining.

options.label: No Piping
options.description: No decorative piping.

options.label: DIY Piping
options.description: Custom piping trim around edges.

options.label: Inner Breast Pocket Area
options.description: Label inside the breast pocket area.

options.label: Side Seam
options.description: Label in the inner side seam.

options.label: Match Fabric
options.description: Buttons matched to fabric — harmonious.

options.label: Appoint Button
options.description: Specify your own button — provide code at checkout.

options.label: Cross
options.description: Traditional cross-thread button sewing.

options.label: Parallel
options.description: Parallel thread button sewing.

options.label: Claw
options.description: Claw-style thread pattern.

options.label: Square
options.description: Square thread pattern — decorative detail.

options.label: Match Buttonhole
options.description: Thread color matches buttonhole.

options.label: DIY Button Thread
options.description: Custom thread color — specify at checkout.

options.label: DIY Placket Last Button
options.description: Custom thread on placket last button.

options.label: Standard

options.label: DIY Lapel Buttonhole
options.description: Custom thread on lapel buttonhole.

options.label: No Pick Stitching
options.description: Clean machine-stitched edges.

options.label: 0.6 cm Pick Stitch
options.description: Hand pick stitch 0.6 cm from edge.

options.label: 0.6 cm Top Stitch
options.description: Machine top stitch 0.6 cm — clean detail.

options.label: Standard
options.description: Standard factory fit.

options.description: Reduce arm hole circumference by 1 cm.

options.label: Deep Stomach Dart
options.description: Deep dart for prominent stomach — better front drape.

options.label: None
options.description: No additional options.

options.label: Satin Button Wrapping
options.description: Satin-wrapped buttons — luxury detail.

options.label: No Seal Stitch on Pocket
options.description: Remove tack stitch on outer pocket.

options.label: Placket with Pad Buttons
options.description: Padded button placket — reinforced.
